description:
## Goal
`fixtures/quicksort/` as the closing artefact for the destructuring epic. The fixture's whole point is to exercise `{lo, hi} = partition(...)` on the hot path of a recursive sort, in interplay with cons patterns in clause heads — the kind of code destructuring exists for.

## Depends on
- T4 (so the parity gates are already in place).

## Fixture content
`fixtures/quicksort/` with the same file shape as `fib_tailrec/`: `input.fz`, `README.md`, `expected.txt`, `expected.clif`, `expected.specs`, `expected.outcomes`.

```
fn append([], ys), do: ys
fn append([h | t], ys), do: [h | append(t, ys)]

fn partition(_, [], lo, hi), do: {lo, hi}
fn partition(p, [h | t], lo, hi) do
  if h < p do partition(p, t, [h | lo], hi)
  else partition(p, t, lo, [h | hi]) end
end

fn qsort([]), do: []
fn qsort([p | rest]) do
  {lo, hi} = partition(p, rest, [], [])
  append(qsort(lo), [p | qsort(hi)])
end

fn main() do
  print(qsort([3, 1, 4, 1, 5, 9, 2, 6, 5, 3, 5]))
end
```

## Acceptance
- Output: a sorted list.
- `expected.clif` shows projection-only lowering for `{lo, hi} = partition(...)` — no synthesized `match_error` halt. Visible proof T2 did its job on a real program.
- `expected.outcomes` records T1's exhaustive-arm outcome at the destructure site.
- Fixture passes on all four legs (interp, run, build, repl).

## Done when
- Fixture exists, all snapshots present.
- All tests pass.
title: T5: fixtures/quicksort/ — closing artefact
